title: On the examined life
creator: Socrates
excerpt: The unexamined life is not worth living.

title: On courage
creator: Nelson Mandela
excerpt: I learned that courage was not the absence of fear, but the triumph over it.
attribution: Nelson Mandela, Long Walk to Freedom

title: On imagination
creator: Albert Einstein
excerpt: Imagination is more important than knowledge. Knowledge is limited. Imagination encircles the world.

title: On the present
creator: Buddha
excerpt: Do not dwell in the past, do not dream of the future, concentrate the mind on the present moment.

title: On beauty
creator: Oscar Wilde
excerpt: Beauty is the wonder of wonders. It is only shallow people who do not judge by appearances.
attribution: Oscar Wilde, The Picture of Dorian Gray

title: On persistence
creator: Mary Anne Radmacher
excerpt: Courage doesn't always roar. Sometimes courage is the quiet voice at the end of the day saying, 'I will try again tomorrow.'

title: On reading
creator: Jorge Luis Borges
excerpt: I have always imagined that Paradise will be a kind of library.

title: On art
creator: Pablo Picasso
excerpt: Art washes away from the soul the dust of everyday life.

title: On simplicity
creator: Henry David Thoreau
excerpt: Our life is frittered away by detail. Simplify, simplify.

title: On wonder
creator: Rachel Carson
excerpt: Those who dwell among the beauties and mysteries of the earth are never alone or weary of life.
attribution: Rachel Carson, The Sense of Wonder

title: On kindness
creator: Aesop
excerpt: No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted.
attribution: Aesop, The Lion and the Mouse

title: On time
creator: Seneca
excerpt: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a lot of it.
attribution: Seneca, On the Shortness of Life

title: On light
creator: Emily Dickinson
excerpt: Forever is composed of nows.

title: On truth
creator: Maya Angelou
excerpt: There is no greater agony than bearing an untold story inside you.
attribution: Maya Angelou, I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings

title: On the sea
creator: Virginia Woolf
excerpt: The very stone one kicks with one's boot will outlast Shakespeare.

title: On hope
creator: Emily Dickinson
excerpt: Hope is the thing with feathers that perches in the soul.

title: On creation
creator: Mary Shelley
excerpt: Nothing is so painful to the human mind as a great and sudden change.

title: On freedom
creator: Simone de Beauvoir
excerpt: One is not born, but rather becomes, a woman.
attribution: Simone de Beauvoir, The Second Sex

title: On nature
creator: John Muir
excerpt: In every walk with nature one receives far more than he seeks.

title: On love
creator: Leo Tolstoy
excerpt: All happy families are alike; each unhappy family is unhappy in its own way.

title: On silence
creator: Rainer Maria Rilke
excerpt: Let everything happen to you: beauty and terror. Just keep going. No feeling is final.
attribution: Rainer Maria Rilke, Go to the Limits of Your Longing

title: On wisdom
creator: Confucius
excerpt: It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.

title: On the stars
creator: Carl Sagan
excerpt: Somewhere, something incredible is waiting to be known.

title: On writing
creator: Annie Dillard
excerpt: How we spend our days is, of course, how we spend our lives.

title: On memory
creator: Marcel Proust
excerpt: The real voyage of discovery consists not in seeking new landscapes, but in having new eyes.

title: On the soul
creator: Khalil Gibran
excerpt: Your living is determined not so much by what life brings to you as by the attitude you bring to life.

title: On dawn
creator: Rumi
excerpt: The morning breeze has secrets to tell you. Do not go back to sleep.

title: On craft
creator: Leonardo da Vinci
excerpt: Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.

title: On the journey
creator: T.S. Eliot
excerpt: We shall not cease from exploration, and the end of all our exploring will be to arrive where we started.

title: On seeing
creator: Marcel Proust
excerpt: The only true voyage of discovery would be not to visit strange lands but to possess other eyes.
attribution: Marcel Proust, Remembrance of Things Past
